title: System for evacuating one or more mobile robots
abstract: There is provided a system and method for evacuating one or more mobile robots from a confined area. The system and method involve one or more mobile robots equipped with sensors or receivers for receiving evacuation commands directing the one or more mobile robots to leave the evacuation area and enter a location outside the evacuation area.
claims: Claims 1. A system for evacuating one or more mobile robots operating in one or more zones in an area, said system comprising: the one or more mobile robots capable of receiving one or more evacuation commands, wherein the one or more evacuation commands comprise information about one or more zones to be evacuated, wherein the system defines an evacuation action to be performed at the one or more zones to be evacuated, said evacuation action directs the one or more mobile robots operating in the one or more zones to be evacuated to leave the one or more zones to be evacuated and enter a location outside the one or more zones to be evacuated in response to the one or more evacuation commands. 2. The system according to claim 1 , wherein the one or more mobile robots is/are autonomous mobile robots. 3. The system according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the one or more mobile robots are controlled by a robot management system. 4. The system according to claim 3, wherein the robot management system is located remote from the one or more mobile robots. 5. The system according to claim 4, wherein the robot management system communicates with the one or more mobile robots via a wireless communication . 6. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the location outside the evacuation area is a specific location inside or outside the confined area. 7. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the robot management system is configured to process information from the one or more mobile robots, said information comprising status on which of the robots are inside or outside the evacuation area. 8. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the robot management system is configured to process commands from an external source, such as an actuator or an ERP system, said commands comprising activation of alarms. 9. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more mobile robots or the management system is configured to determine the shortest or fastest path out of the evacuation area. 10. The system according to claim 9, wherein the one or more mobile robots or the management system based on the determined shortest or fastest path executes an exit out of the evacuation area. 1 1. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more mobile robots is restricted from entering the evacuation area during a prescribed time. 12. The system according to claim 6, wherein the one or more mobile robots are programmed to move to an alternative location if the specific location is occupied or if the path thereto has been obstructed. 13. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the specific location is selected from the group consisting of dedicated parking lots for the one or more mobile robots, specific compartments, and docking stations. 14. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein information obtained by the one or more mobile robots during the evacuation is deleted upon completion of the evacuation thereby resetting the one or more mobile robots to a situation before the evacuation. 15. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the confined area is a building. 16. The system according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the evacuation zones are selected from the group consisting of corridors, fire emergency zones, emergency exits, fire doors, and elevators. 17. A method for evacuating one or more mobile robots from specific zones in an area, said method comprising the steps of: emitting one or more evacuation commands from a source emitting said commands to the one or more mobile robots equipped with sensors or receivers for receiving the one or more evacuation commands, whereby the one or more of the mobile robots evacuate from one or more specific evacuation zones; wherein the method defines an evacuation action to be performed at a zone at which to perform the evacuation for the one or more mobile robots, said evacuation action directs the one or more mobile robots to leave the evacuation area and enter a location outside the evacuation area in response to the one or more evacuation commands. 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein the one or more mobile robots are controlled by a robot management system. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein the robot management system is located remote from the one or more mobile robots. 20. The method according to any one of the claims 17 to 19, wherein the one or more mobile robots are commanded to leave the evacuation area and enter a location outside the evacuation area in response to the one or more evacuation commands.
